parsed_review.summary: CP00-898 is a descriptor-only RP30 People HR Evidence (embedded HRX) contract baseline continuation: 10 units, risk A, RP30.P00.M06.S01-RP30.P00.M09.S01, distribution {scope:3, admission:3, non-goal:3, decision:1}. The pack-level claim under review is production_ready_flag=hrx_contract_acceptance_baseline_continuation_descriptor_verified with production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false; that claim is fully supported. Evidence is internally consistent across the live plan, manifest, baseline, contract projection, registry/validators, and tests: the live closeout-pack-plan.json lists CP00-898 as the first planned pack with matching range/risk/unit_count and the same included units, and unit/deliverable/micro-title distributions match HRX_CP898_REQUIREMENTS. All 15 mandatory artifacts exist; every referenced validation npm script (closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack/test/validate/spec:requirements/weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane plus rp30/hrx ledger validators) is defined, and packages/hrx/test/*.test.js is included in `npm test`. command-evidence records initial targeted validations at exit 0 with the full deterministic ladder correctly deferred to after review (commit_policy=blocked_until_review_and_full_validation). No-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries are enforced in code: packages/hrx and the validate script are pure deepFrozen descriptor generators and read-only validators with no writeFile/network/child_process/exec/process.env, and per-row plus top-level attestations (descriptor_only=true; runtime_execution/payroll/HR-AI-judgment/permission-write/audit-write/product-state-write/runtime-receipt/real-employee-candidate-payroll-client-data/credential = false) are present; no PII or secrets found. Authority boundary holds: Claude is read-only (claude_packet.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), human final approval is required for runtime opening (deferred to CP00-899), local validation does not claim enterprise trust, and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review/evidence packets (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. Review-hardening checks pass: auth preflight succeeded (exit 0, 1432-byte valid JSON stdout, no stderr), and the request is claude-opus-4-8/max/read-only with Read,Grep,Glob only and a 2429-byte prompt under the size limit. One non-blocking P3 provenance nit carried over from CP897: the contract's generated_by names a validate-only script while the actual projection generator (createHrxPeopleContractProjection) is unwired. No P0/P1/P2 findings. blocks_goal_closeout is false per scope (pack claims neither runtime nor enterprise trust); final human/enterprise-trust approval remains outside this receipt.

findings.title: Contract generated_by names a validate-only script; projection generator is unwired
findings.details: The committed contract sets generated_by="scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs", but that script only resolves the contract path (scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs:17) and readJson()s it for validation (line 19); it performs no filesystem write. The canonical projection generator createHrxPeopleContractProjection() (packages/hrx/src/registry.js:510), whose output the JSON mirrors, is exported but not imported by any emitting script (grep finds only its definition), so contracts/hrx-people-contract.json is effectively a hand-maintained static artifact with a cosmetic provenance label. This is identical to CP897-P3-01 and is purely a documentation/maintainability inaccuracy: it does not violate the descriptor-only, no-write, no-real-data, or no-runtime boundaries, and the file still validates cleanly against the validators.
findings.recommendation: Either add a generator script that emits the contract from createHrxPeopleContractProjection() and point generated_by at it, or change generated_by to indicate the file is hand-maintained and validated by scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs. Non-blocking.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking P3; no effect on descriptor-only boundary, no-write/no-real-data posture, or production-ready gating. Safe to proceed to closeout once the full deterministic validation ladder passes.
